vehicle.label: Tiltrotor VTOL Hybrid
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing VTOL with 2 tilting rotor nacelles, BVLOS long-range capable

parts.label: Center Fuselage Spine
parts.spec: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

parts.label: Main Wing (2-piece)
parts.spec: Straight wing, 4S NACA 2412, dual 15mm CFRP spar, 900mm half-span, 160mm chord, 3-layer CFRP skin

parts.label: Nacelle Assembly x 2
parts.spec: Tilting nacelle assembly, 0-90deg tilt axis, 6309 bearing set, servo-driven worm gear, motor and ESC pocket

parts.label: Tilt Servo + Linkage x 2
parts.spec: 25kg-cm DS3218 servo per nacelle, titanium pushrod, dual ball joints, tilt range 0-93deg, crash-tolerant

parts.label: V-Tail x 2 (Ruddervator)
parts.spec: +-35deg V-tail ruddervator pair, MG996R servo each, 30deg dihedral, CFRP sandwich panel, hinge piano wire

parts.label: Landing Gear (Tri)
parts.spec: Tricycle landing gear, spring-steel skid arms, 55mm dia wheels, CG at 1/3 chord, 200mm wheelbase

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide-In)
parts.spec: Belly slide-in battery bay, 6S 8000mAh Li-ion pack, Velcro + strap dual retention, CG position 28% MAC

parts.label: Payload Nose Bay
parts.spec: Interchangeable nose payload bay, Day/IR camera gimbal or survey sensor, 80x60x50mm, 300g payload limit

parts.label: Tilt-Axis Harness + Datum Service Bay
parts.spec: Tiltrotor integration_service module with tilt-axis harness_channel, slack_loop, interface_datum, service_hatch, connector keying, and nacelle-to-wing load-path inspection access

generated_parts.label: Center Fuselage Spine

verify.value: All required fields and items are present.
verify.rule: Ensure all schema fields and items are included.

verify.value: Mass estimate is provided and within expected range.
verify.rule: Ensure cad_brief.mass_est_g is a numeric grams value.

verify.value: All P0 internal_feature_targets are present in part_tree and geometry_ops.
verify.rule: Ensure P0 internal_feature_targets appear as part_tree children and geometry_ops ids or targets.

verify.value: All interfaces have reciprocal naming and details.
verify.rule: Ensure interfaces have exact neighboring subsystem labels and matching connection_type, DOF, clearance, and required_feature.

verify.value: All critical joints have visible locking/preload evidence.
verify.rule: Ensure critical joints have visible insert/washer/locking/preload evidence and torque_Nm or preload intent.
Include fixture_points, thermal_zones, and pressure_faces for downstream CFD/FEA.

risk.desc: Potential thermal stress on avionics bay due to heat dissipation.
risk.mit: Increase heat sink area and consider thermal insulation.

risk.desc: Camera window may be prone to damage from environmental factors.
risk.mit: Use reinforced TPU for the camera window to improve durability.

risk.desc: Landing gear mount may experience excessive stress during landing.
risk.mit: Review landing gear mount design and consider adding reinforcement.

risk.desc: GPS boom mount may not provide sufficient clearance for maintenance.
risk.mit: Increase clearance around GPS boom mount to allow easier access.

risk.desc: V-tail mount may not be adequately secured during high-g maneuvers.
risk.mit: Review V-tail mount design and consider adding additional fasteners.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

physics_paths.load_path: Wing spar loads into fuselage spine via spar_socket
physics_paths.thermal_path: Heat from avionics bay dissipates through fuselage_spine
physics_paths.pressure_path: Belly camera window maintains pressure seal
physics_paths.electrical_path: Avionics bay electrical harnesses routed through harness_passthrough
physics_paths.service_path: GPS boom mount allows front GPS boom to be accessed for maintenance

adjacent_interfaces.assembly_order_hint: install wing spar into spar_socket before attaching fuselage_spine to wing
attach avionics_bay_mount to fuselage_spine before installing avionics bay
attach landing_gear_mount to fuselage_spine before installing landing gear

manufacturing_strategy.datum: Primary locating faces, rail guides, axis rings, spar sockets, or mating pads
manufacturing_strategy.support: Support structures for FDM, toolpaths for CNC
manufacturing_strategy.inspection: CMM for dimensional accuracy, visual inspection for surface finish

verify.value: All required features and interfaces are present in the part_tree and geometry_ops.
verify.rule: Each part_tree child must have at least 4 dedicated geometry_ops that reference it by id or target name.

verify.value: All P0 internal_feature_targets appear as part_tree children and geometry_ops ids or targets.
verify.rule: P0 internal_feature_targets must appear as part_tree children and geometry_ops ids or targets.

verify.value: All interfaces have named exact neighboring subsystem labels and choose connection_type + DOF + clearance_mm + required_feature.
verify.rule: Name exact neighboring subsystem labels when they mate, and choose connection_type + DOF + clearance_mm + required_feature so the neighbor can declare the same joint family back.

verify.value: Visible insert/washer/locking/preload evidence and torque_Nm or preload intent.
verify.rule: Critical joints need visible insert/washer/locking/preload evidence and torque_Nm or preload intent.
verify must include a boundary_conditions object with fixture_points, thermal_zones, and pressure_faces.

risk.desc: Potential risk of thermal expansion causing misalignment at high altitudes.
risk.mit: Include thermal expansion joints and conduct thermal analysis to ensure proper fit.

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient stiffness leading to structural failure under high loads.
risk.mit: Conduct finite element analysis to verify stiffness and strength requirements.

risk.desc: Risk of improper fastener locking leading to loosening over time.
risk.mit: Ensure all fasteners have proper locking mechanisms and torque specifications.

risk.desc: Risk of inadequate service access for maintenance and repair.
risk.mit: Design service access panels and ensure they are easily removable.

risk.desc: Risk of pressure imbalance causing structural deformation.
risk.mit: Conduct pressure analysis and ensure proper sealing and venting.

risk.desc: Potential thermal stress at bearing seat due to high operating temperatures.
risk.mit: Implement thermal vias and cooling channels to manage heat dissipation.

risk.desc: Risk of misalignment during assembly due to complex assembly sequence.
risk.mit: Provide detailed assembly instructions and alignment tools for critical steps.

risk.desc: Risk of inadequate clearance for motor and ESC access during maintenance.
risk.mit: Ensure sufficient clearance and easy access for maintenance and replacement.

risk.desc: Risk of improper fit due to tolerance stack-up not being accounted for.
risk.mit: Review and adjust tolerance stack-up to ensure proper fit and function.

risk.desc: Risk of structural failure due to high aerodynamic loads on the nacelle shell.
risk.mit: Conduct stress analysis and reinforce critical areas as needed.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Tilting nacelle assembly, 0-90deg tilt axis, 6309 bearing set, servo-driven worm gear, motor and ESC pocket
physics_paths: load_path: rotor thrust loads routed through bearing seats into wing structure
flow_path: cooling airflow from wing leading edge to nacelle motor and ESC
thermal_path: heat dissipation from motor and ESC to wing structure via thermal vias
motion_path: tilt axis allows nacelle to rotate from 0 to 90 degrees
pressure_path: aerodynamic pressure loads on nacelle shell transferred to wing structure via mounting brackets
service_path: access to motor and ESC for maintenance and replacement through hinged access panels

generated_parts.label: Tilt Servo + Linkage x 2
generated_parts.spec: 25kg-cm DS3218 servo per nacelle, titanium pushrod, dual ball joints, tilt range 0-93deg, crash-tolerant

requirements: Design a tilt servo linkage subsystem for a tiltrotor VTOL hybrid with high-altitude mapping capabilities.
Subsystem must include a tilt axis, bearing seats, servo linkage, clearance arc, and servo mount.
Ensure subsystem is crash-tolerant and suitable for long-range BVLOS missions.
Subsystem must be compatible with CFRP, Al, and TPU materials, manufactured via FDM and CNC processes.

Material: CFRP + Al + TPU, Process: FDM + CNC
Evidence features: interface_datum, harness_channel, service_hatch, tilt_axis, service_window, harness_path
